key_insight: MTP rollout speed in RL is limited by entropy growth, and rejection sampling with an end-to-end TV loss makes speculative decoding stay effective through training.
engineering_relevance: This enables much faster RL post-training pipelines for large LLMs, with up to 1.8x end-to-end acceleration and improved throughput on reasoning, code, and agentic workloads.
method: Analyze how RL entropy fluctuations bound MTP acceptance rate
Use probabilistic rejection sampling instead of greedy draft sampling
Train with an end-to-end TV loss to directly optimize multi-step acceptance
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning, code generation, and agentic tasks, the method improved acceptance rates by about 10%, reached up to 95% acceptance, and delivered up to 25% extra inference throughput.
watch_item: The speedup depends on entropy behavior during RL and on integrating rejection sampling correctly, so gains may be smaller if the model or training setup differs substantially.
paper_title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
